items.notes: Rust uses bitflags! macro: WindowFlags struct with MODAL, UNDECORATED, POPUP, MAXIMIZED, FULLSCREEN, AUTO_DELETE bits.

items.superseding_construct: Rust Drop trait handles destruction automatically
items.notes: No explicit destructor needed in Rust.
C++ cross-pointer idiom not needed in Rust.
No signal infrastructure for window flags changes in Rust equivalent; winit events cover this.
WM resource names are X11-specific; not modelled in Rust/winit.
WindowPort abstraction is collapsed into ZuiWindow itself.
ZuiWindow::signal_closing() returns the SignalId for the caller to fire.
X11 geometry string parsing not implemented in Rust.
winit does not expose border/decoration thickness; no Rust equivalent.
ZuiWindow::set_title(&str) sets title on winit window.
The WindowPort abstraction layer is not needed; Rust ZuiWindow talks directly to winit.
ZuiWindow::set_window_icon updates the winit icon directly.
ZuiWindow::signal_closing() returns SignalId for caller to fire.
Port abstraction collapsed; separate methods handle each combo.
WindowStateSaver struct with WindowGeometry data model.
WindowStateSaver::cycle(window, focused) - saves geometry on focus/change.
WindowGeometry: x, y, width, height, maximized, fullscreen.
InputKey enum covers all EM_KEY_* values.

items.superseding_construct: Option<InputKey> (None) or absence of key in Rust
items.notes: Rust uses Option instead of a sentinel value.
InputKey::MouseLeft, MouseRight, MouseMiddle, WheelUp, WheelDown, WheelLeft, WheelRight, MouseX1, MouseX2.

EM_KEY_PRINT, EM_KEY_PAUSE, EM_KEY_MENU, EM_KEY_INSERT, EM_KEY_DELETE, EM_KEY_BACKSPACE, EM_KEY_TAB, EM_KEY_ENTER, EM_KEY_ESCAPE, EM_KEY_SPACE
items.notes: InputKey::Print, Pause, Menu, Insert, Delete, Backspace, Tab, Enter, Escape, Space.

items.symbol: EM_KEY_0 through EM_KEY_9, EM_KEY_A through EM_KEY_Z

items.superseding_construct: InputEvent struct initialised directly or via factory methods
items.notes: No Setup method needed; Rust uses struct literal init.
InputState::touches() iteration with find by id.
InputState::set_touch(id, x, y) inserts or updates.

items.ambiguity_reason: No direct clear_touches method; InputState::remove_touch is per-id, or touches could be cleared by removing all. No explicit bulk clear in the Rust implementation.

items.notes: InputState::is_no_mod, is_shift_mod, is_ctrl_mod, is_alt_mod, is_meta_mod, is_shift_ctrl_mod, is_shift_alt_mod, is_shift_meta_mod, is_ctrl_alt_mod, is_ctrl_meta_mod, is_alt_meta_mod, is_shift_ctrl_alt_mod, is_shift_ctrl_meta_mod, is_shift_alt_meta_mod, is_ctrl_alt_meta_mod, is_shift_ctrl_alt_meta_mod.
Rust uses a HashSet rather than a raw bitfield array.
Hotkey::parse(s) -> Option<Hotkey> (returns None on failure instead of throwing).
No Display or to_string for Hotkey in the Rust implementation.
Rust uses Option rather than an invalid sentinel state.
No ClearModifiers helper in Rust Hotkey; caller constructs a new Hotkey.
No incremental modifier builder in Rust Hotkey.
No setter; Rust Hotkey fields are assigned directly.
Cursor::ResizeEW (closest match; Rust enum is more granular).
Cursor is a Copy enum; variants are used directly.
No integer conversion needed; Rust pattern-matches on Cursor variants.
Cursor does not implement Display or a to_string method in Rust.
Version is managed by Cargo in Rust.
Rust programs access version via Cargo env macros.
C++ binary compatibility checks are not needed in Rust.

items.superseding_construct: Rust std locale handling (or no explicit init needed)
items.notes: Rust does not require explicit locale initialisation for UTF-8.
Irrelevant in Rust; String is always UTF-8.

items.superseding_construct: Rust strings are always UTF-8; no multibyte state needed
items.notes: C locale multibyte state is irrelevant in Rust.
Rust stdlib provides full UTF-8 handling.
Rust uses the log crate for structured logging.
Module-prefixed logging via the log crate.
Debug log filtering is handled by env_logger or similar at runtime.
Rust panic or explicit process exit.
No GUI-based fatal error dialog in Rust.

items.superseding_construct: Rust ownership model / absence of Copy/Clone
items.notes: Rust types are non-copy by default.

items.superseding_construct: Rust enums with no variants (uninhabited types) or modules without structs
items.notes: Not needed in Rust; free functions can be placed in modules.
No emAlignment equivalent found in Rust source.
No alignment string serialisation in Rust.
Rust has built-in sized integer types.
Rust primitive types expose min/max constants.
Rust stdlib provides integer parsing and formatting.
Platform adaptation macros are replaced by Rust's own mechanisms.

items.superseding_construct: Rust Result<T, E> and custom error enums
items.notes: Rust uses Result rather than exception-based error handling.

items.superseding_construct: hostname crate or std::process / platform syscalls
items.notes: Not in Rust stdlib; would use an external crate. No Rust equivalent present in codebase.
Rust std provides CPU feature detection macros.
CPU TSC read is debug-only; no Rust equivalent present in codebase.
Rust stdlib provides OS error text.
No direct Rust stdlib equivalent; try-open approach.

items.superseding_construct: Check filename starts with '.' on Unix

items.notes: Rust std::fs provides a cleaner iterator API.

items.superseding_construct: std::fs::copy() for files; recursive copy needs manual impl or crate
items.notes: Rust stdlib only has file copy; tree copy needs manual recursion.
Dynamic library loading not present in Rust codebase; Rust uses static linking or the libloading crate.
Rust uses the rand crate for pseudo-random number generation.
No checksum/hash utility functions found in Rust codebase.
No Tunnel panel found in Rust source (checked widget/ and panel/).
No Tunnel panel in Rust. C++ deprecated with 'final'.
